blocks: AT&T Fiber, HBO Max Upsell

Developed responsive HTML email template for AT&T Fiber's HBO Max bundle campaign promoting entertainment-grade internet with included streaming benefits. Built promotional email featuring animated GIF hero image with visual motion effects showcasing AT&T Fiber and HBO Max co-branding.

Implemented multi-section informational layout highlighting 1 GIG internet speeds starting at $39.99/month, 1000Mbps connection capabilities, and unlimited data ($30 value) bundled with HBO Max access. Coded detailed service comparison messaging emphasizing bandwidth advantages over cable providers (Xfinity, Spectrum, Cox) with supporting footnotes and disclaimers. Developed mobile-responsive design with optimized image performance, gradient CTAs, and featured content grid showcasing Westworld and Friends programming to drive fiber upgrade conversions.

Developed responsive HTML email template for Aimmune Therapeutics' Palforzia pharmaceutical marketing campaign, targeting families enrolled in peanut allergy treatment programs. Built patient journey email featuring testimonial video content and educational resources including prescribing information, medication guides, and safety documentation links.

Implemented personalized for patient messaging while ensuring strict pharmaceutical compliance and accessibility standards with comprehensive alt-text attributes. Optimized image-to-text ratios for enhanced deliverability and tested mobile-responsive layout with image swap functionality across all major email clients. Maintained regulatory-compliant footer elements including privacy notices, unsubscribe mechanisms, and required pharmaceutical disclosures for HIPAA-sensitive patient communications.
DIRECTV and NBC partnership

This project was a static promotional email for the DIRECTV and NBC partnership, built to showcase network content with a strong, image forward layout while staying dependable across inboxes. I handled the build end to end, translating the creative into a responsive template that preserved the visual impact on desktop and mobile, while also meeting accessibility requirements through proper alt text and clear reading order.

The email was tuned for performance and deliverability by optimizing image assets, keeping a healthy image to text balance, and using mobile image swaps where needed. It was tested across major email clients and devices to ensure consistent rendering, resulting in a lightweight, standards compliant template that balanced branding, accessibility, and reliability.

The Podcast Fellowship
Product Manager

I've been the fractional Technical Product Manager for The Podcast Fellowship since 2021. TPF is a non-profit that pays students to listen to educational podcasts—pretty cool mission. I oversee their offshore dev team and manage the web application that runs the whole program.

My role is different from my usual email marketing and CRM work. I handle technical architecture decisions, prioritize the product roadmap, and translate between the non-technical nonprofit leadership and the development team. I guide frontend development, optimize user experience, and make sure the platform stays reliable as the program grows. It's been a great experience managing a consumer web app outside my typical marketing technology wheelhouse.
Yogaglo website and application
From 2016 - 2108, I was a front-end developer at Glo, and developed the UI for YogaGlo's web application, a full-stack platform built with Ember.js and Express.
Back in 2016, streaming exercise classes online was cutting edge!

I worked on multiple features including the login page highlighting their "Notes" feature, user dashboard, and class browsing interface. Built responsive, mobile-first interfaces using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript that performed consistently across all devices and browsers. Focused on creating an intuitive user experience for yoga practitioners to access classes, save personal notes, and filter content by their preferences.
American Express Financial Calculators - Auto Loan

I was the lead developer across a suite of about seven interactive financial calculators for American Express Credit Intel, including the Auto Loan Calculator. I built these tools with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, focusing on fast, responsive UI, accessible form controls, and a consistent interaction pattern so users could quickly understand outcomes without friction across desktop and mobile.

For the Auto Loan Calculator specifically, I implemented the core amortization and payment logic and wired it to a clean input experience with strong validation and guardrails. That included handling common edge cases like zero down payment, short terms, very low or high APRs, and rounding consistency so the monthly payment, total interest, and overall cost stay accurate and stable as users adjust inputs.

Emory Healthcare - Custom-Built SFMC Calendar
Built with: Salesforce Marketing Cloud, custom Cloud Pages, SSJS, AMPscript, SQL (Query Activities), and the SFMC REST and SOAP APIs.

I built an internal calendar app that gives the marketing team a single view of every email and journey going out across the health system. Before this, there was no easy way to see what was scheduled, what overlapped, or which patients were getting hit by multiple campaigns at once. Now it all lives on one screen.

It runs entirely inside Salesforce Marketing Cloud as a custom Cloud Page. I designed the whole data pipeline: nightly automations pull journey and campaign data into cached data extensions, and a one click Sync refreshes it on demand. A separate admin page lets the team control how everything displays (service lines, color coding, categories, manual campaigns, exclusions) without touching any code.

The most interesting part was the overlap and saturation logic: figuring out, across thousands of sends, which patients were receiving too many messages in a given window, and surfacing that clearly on the calendar. I also wired in campaign metrics, so the team can click any day and see how a campaign actually performed on opens, clicks, and sends.

Brilliant Earth - Abandoned Cart Analysis

This project focused on clarifying the business rules behind the entire campaign. I worked through how the system decides who qualifies, what counts as abandonment, how session timing affects triggers, and how purchases or recent messages suppress sends. While the creative supported dynamic products and pricing, the core work was making sure the rules reflected real shopping behavior and matched stakeholder expectations.

On the technical and strategy side, we pressure tested edge cases like multiple cart events in a day, switching products, and overlaps with other campaigns. We clarified when a series should restart, when a newer cart should replace an older one, and when a customer should be suppressed entirely. The result was a scalable, frequency safe campaign with clear, documented logic for who gets the message, which product appears, and when it should or should not send.

Emory Healthcare - Patient Reengagement Campaign
Built with: Salesforce Marketing Cloud, SQL (Query Activities), Journey Builder, SSJS, AMPscript, custom Cloud Pages, and the SFMC REST and SOAP APIs.

I built an automated campaign to win back lapsed patients, people who had engaged with Emory before but had gone quiet. The goal was to reach them again with something relevant to their care, not just a generic blast.

The core is a data pipeline I designed in SQL: it looks at engagement history, identifies who has lapsed, and rebuilds a fresh audience on a schedule. Each patient is then routed by service line based on the care area of their last interaction, so someone who last saw orthopedics gets different messaging than someone who saw cardiology. From there they flow into a Journey Builder journey with A/B tested emails, so we could actually learn what brings people back rather than guess.

I built the whole thing with a full test harness too: every production piece (the audience, the journey, the emails) has a parallel test version, and the entire flow runs end to end in test before anything reaches a real patient. The screenshot is a data flow page I built to map every step of the pipeline and its status on one screen, so the team can see the whole system at a glance.

United Health Care
Design, Development & QA

This project demonstrates my ability to take a basic Word document brief with brand assets and transform it into a fully designed and then coded email. I handled the process end-to-end: interpreting the brief, applying design principles, and developing a responsive, cross-client-compatible HTML email.
Interpreting the Brief
Extracted key messaging and visual hierarchy from the Word document.
Identified essential content sections like the headline, callouts (e.g., Service Heroes), and closing remarks for emphasis.
Design Stage
Visual Layout: Created a clear and engaging layout based on the content, emphasizing headers, quotes, and recognition sections.
Brand Consistency: Applied UnitedHealthcare's brand guidelines, including typography, colors, and image placement.
Mobile-First Approach: Designed with responsive layouts to ensure readability and functionality across devices.
Development Stage
HTML Coding: Hand-coded the email to reflect the design, ensuring clean, maintainable, and responsive code.
Cross-Client Compatibility: Addressed quirks in email clients like Outlook using techniques such as inline styles and VML for background images.
Accessibility Compliance: Added alt text for images and structured the content for screen readers to ensure usability for all audiences.
Quality Assurance
Rendering Tests: Used Email on Acid to validate design consistency across 90+ email clients.
Functionality Tests: Verified all links and interactive elements worked correctly.
Performance Optimization: Minimized load time by compressing assets and cleaning up code.

T-Mobile HINT Campaign

I was a campaign manager for T-Mobile’s 5G Home Internet offering - a coordinated six-touch sequence across email and SMS. Email Journeys and Cadence:
Designed structured email journeys (e.g., T1–T6), balancing engagement frequency with customer experience.
Aligned messaging and timing with customer lifecycle stages to maximize impact.
Campaign Strategy:
Contributed to crafting strategic messaging for email and SMS touchpoints to drive engagement and conversions.
Collaborated with cross-functional teams to ensure campaigns met broader marketing objectives.
Data Pipelines:
Oversaw data flow from multiple sources into SFMC, ensuring accurate segmentation and personalization.
Managed tools like Control-M to monitor and maintain pipeline integrity for seamless campaign execution.
KPI Tracking and Optimization:
Monitored key metrics like delivery rate, bounce rate, and engagement to measure campaign success.
Used performance data to refine future strategies and improve ROI.

AT&T and Spotify partnership

This project was a static promotional email announcing the AT&T and Spotify Premium partnership, designed to highlight the offer with a clean, typography driven layout and strong brand presence from both partners. I translated the creative into a responsive email that held up across desktop and mobile, while making sure the message stayed clear, readable, and accessible through proper alt text and structured content flow.

The build focused on efficiency and inbox reliability, with optimized image assets, careful image to text balance, and mobile specific image swaps where needed. The template was tested across major email clients and devices to ensure consistent rendering, resulting in a lightweight, deliverable friendly email that balanced performance, accessibility, and visual impact.
Blue Shield of California

This project covered the 2023 AEP campaign for Blue Shield of California, built in Salesforce Marketing Cloud with multiple versions in both English and Spanish. I implemented the emails using SFMC content blocks and AMPscript, structuring the templates to support dynamic messaging while keeping the layouts consistent across languages and audiences. The work focused on making the campaign flexible for marketing while still maintaining strict control over branding and layout.

On the technical side, I designed the templates to handle conditional content, language switching, and reusable components without duplicating code. I tested the builds across major email clients to ensure reliable rendering and predictable fallbacks, especially for image and text-heavy sections. The final result was a scalable campaign setup that made it easy to manage variations while keeping production and QA overhead low.

Spanish version of AT&T Samsung Galaxy partnership email


Developed responsive HTML email template for AT&T's Spanish-language marketing campaign promoting the Samsung Galaxy S10 product launch to Hispanic audiences. Collaborated with marketing teams to localize product messaging and partnership content, showcasing flagship device features including professional-grade camera, ultrasonic fingerprint sensor, intelligent battery, and wireless PowerShare capabilities.

American Express Financial Calculators - Home Loan

I was the lead developer across a suite of about seven financial calculators for American Express Credit Intel, including the Home Equity Calculator. Each tool was built with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, with a shared focus on clear UX, responsive layout, and accessibility so the experiences worked consistently across devices and input methods.

For the Home Equity Calculator specifically, I implemented the UI and calculation logic that estimates available equity from home value and mortgage balance, with strong input validation and edge case handling for scenarios like low or negative equity. More broadly, I helped design a reusable pattern for these tools so calculations, formatting, and error handling stayed consistent across the entire calculator suite as requirements and compliance reviews evolved.
PNC Bank Quarterly Newsletter

Developed responsive HTML email template in Salesforce Marketing Cloud for PNC Capital Advisors' quarterly institutional newsletter. Collaborated with content strategists and designers to build modular editorial layout featuring multiple article sections, thought leadership content, and investment insights.

Coded reusable content blocks with AMPscript personalization and implemented mobile-responsive design with advanced media queries for optimal readability across devices. Ensured cross-client compatibility through extensive testing and maintained strict accessibility standards with proper alt-text and semantic HTML structure for professional financial services audience.
Emory Healthcare - KPI Dashboard
Built with: Salesforce Marketing Cloud, custom Cloud Pages, SSJS, AMPscript, SQL (Query Activities), and the SFMC REST and SOAP APIs.

Salesforce Marketing Cloud does have built in reporting, but it's clunky and not something the team wanted to open every day. So I built a clean, simple dashboard on top of the same data: one screen that shows how their email is actually performing (sends, opens, clicks, bounces, and unsubscribes), rolled up by campaign, category, and service line. Instead of digging through native reports, they get one always current view of the whole program.

It runs entirely inside Salesforce Marketing Cloud as a custom Cloud Page. I built the data pipeline behind it: nightly automations aggregate raw send and tracking data into a summary data extension, and a Sync button refreshes the most recent activity on demand. A separate admin page lets the team map campaigns to categories and service lines and manage exclusions, so the rollups stay accurate even as campaign naming changes over time.

The hardest part was making the numbers trustworthy at scale. I reworked the nightly refresh to process data in weekly chunks and switched it to an upsert model, which stopped it from double counting and from failing on high volume days. The frontend is a single Cloud Page I designed for a clean, focused UI, rendered with AMPscript and SSJS, with the interactive metrics, filters, and drilldowns built in vanilla JavaScript, CSS, and HTML.

AT&TTV NOW/HBOMAX

This project was a static promotional email for the AT&T TV Now and HBO Max partnership, built to showcase a content grid of featured shows while keeping the message clear and visually punchy. I translated the creative into a responsive email layout that held its structure across desktop and mobile, and ensured accessibility through proper alt text, readable typography, and a logical content order.

On the technical side, the template was optimized for performance and deliverability by compressing and balancing image assets, keeping a healthy image to text ratio, and supporting mobile specific image swaps. It was tested across major email clients and devices, resulting in a reliable, lightweight campaign email that balanced strong branding with accessibility and inbox friendly performance.
Blue Shield Medicare Informational email

This project was an informational follow-up email for Blue Shield Medicare, sent after a user requested educational materials about Medicare and retirement benefits. The goal was to confirm the request, deliver the promised content, and guide recipients to next steps without feeling salesy or overwhelming. I built the template in Salesforce Marketing Cloud using reusable content blocks so the team could easily adapt it for similar post-request or confirmation scenarios.

Because this was a regulated healthcare communication, the build emphasized compliance and clarity alongside visual polish. The template accounted for CAN-SPAM and accessibility requirements, included clear contact and opt-out sections, and was tested across major email clients using tools like Litmus and Email on Acid. The end result was a reliable, compliant transactional-style email that balanced trust, usability, and maintainability.
Decathlon website - feature development
and bug fixes

I worked as a front-end developer on Decathlon's Shopify e-commerce site back in 2018. Fixed critical bugs in the add-to-cart checkout flow that were impacting conversion rates, and developed several new pages and features for the apparel website. Used Shopify Liquid templating language along with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to build responsive product pages and optimize the user experience across devices.
Instructor up-sell email

This project was an instructor-focused upsell email for YogaGlo, designed to introduce users to teachers they were most likely to enjoy. The first section featured an animated module that rotated through instructor profiles, while the rest of the template supported highlighting individual teachers with clear calls to action. I worked alongside a machine learning engineer who handled the recommendation logic, and my role was to turn those outputs into a dynamic, production-ready email experience.

On the technical side, I built the template with responsive, email-safe HTML and CSS and wired it to consume instructor recommendations from an internal endpoint. I tested the build across major email clients to ensure the animation, layout, and fallbacks behaved predictably on both desktop and mobile.
Dr. Ken Hanson website

Designed and developed complete website for Dr. Ken Hanson, distinguished history professor at The University of Central Florida specializing in bringing history to life through engaging presentations. Created comprehensive brand identity including custom logo design and established visual design system with branded color palette, typography, and style guidelines applied consistently across all site content.

Built responsive website architecture with navigation structure including home, about, media, booking/contact, store, and events sections. Edited hero video content to incorporate branded signature animation at beginning and end, reinforcing professional speaker brand. Developed mobile-responsive layout ensuring optimal viewing experience across all devices while maintaining brand consistency throughout the user journey.
Emory Healthcare

As a Salesforce Marketing Cloud Architect for Emory Healthcare, I coded sql automations, created journeys, and coded emails. The email below is an event communications for Emory Healthcare's Winship Cancer Institute events. Designed and coded dynamic responsive registration and reminder emails in Salesforce Marketing Cloud, and built the supporting Journey Builder logic for confirmations and timed reminders.

I implemented dynamic header imagery that automatically switches between Winship Cancer Institute and standard Emory Healthcare branding based on event type, along with data-driven personalization to ensure attendees received accurate session details and consistent, correct branding across all sends.
American Express Financial Calculators - Debt Consolidation

I was the lead developer across a suite of about seven interactive financial calculators for American Express Credit Intel, including the credit card debt consolidation calculator, a Credit Intel interactive that helps users compare payoff scenarios and understand tradeoffs like interest cost, monthly payment, and timeline. I built the experience end to end with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, focusing on a clean UI, responsive layout, and accessibility so it worked smoothly across devices and was usable with keyboards and screen readers.

The harder part was the math: implementing and validating the financial calculations with careful handling for rounding, input constraints, edge cases, and realistic output formatting that stays consistent as inputs change. I also added guardrails and clear error states to prevent bad inputs, and structured the code to be maintainable as requirements evolved, since these tools tend to get iterative updates once stakeholders see real user behavior.

Marketing email for real estate firm

T-Mobile Campaign Management & Tracking

Provided SQL-driven campaign operations support for T-Mobile's email marketing programs in Salesforce Marketing Cloud. Developed custom SQL queries in Query Studio to analyze and track multi-day treatment campaigns, enabling marketers to verify audience counts, identify duplicate sends, and validate segmentation logic before deployment.

Built queries to count distinct treatment audiences across day-of, 30-day, and 90-day follow-up journeys, helping marketing teams make data-informed decisions on campaign timing and audience targeting. Performed QA through SQL analysis to prevent duplicate sends and ensure proper customer journey assignment, directly supporting marketers with actionable data insights that improved campaign accuracy and protected deliverability performance across T-Mobile's customer communication programs.
Toyota Mirai Email Campaign

Developed responsive HTML email template for Toyota's Mirai hydrogen fuel cell vehicle campaign promoting zero-emission mobility technology. Collaborated iteratively with design team to address critical accessibility and cross-client compatibility issues including converting text-embedded images to live HTML for screen reader support, replacing gradient backgrounds causing Outlook rendering failures with solid colors, and implementing mobile-responsive stacking for side-by-side layouts.

Built bulletproof CSS buttons replacing image-based CTAs for universal client compatibility. Added comprehensive alt-text attributes for all imagery to ensure ADA compliance. Implemented feature callouts showcasing $15,000 fuel credit, CA HOV carpool sticker eligibility, and extended driving range with icon-based design elements and social media integration.
